text.title: python Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for modern Python 3.11+ services.

rule.statement: Business logic lives in services.py, not views. Services receive domain types (email: str, user: User), not request objects. Wrap multi-step DB operations in @transaction.atomic.
rule.rationale: Logic in views couples to HTTP; services are unit-testable and reusable across web/CLI/cron entry points.

rule.statement: Use asyncio.gather() or TaskGroup for independent concurrent I/O. Sequential awaits run one at a time and waste latency.
rule.rationale: Three `await client.get(url)` calls sequentially take 3x the latency; gather/TaskGroup runs them concurrently.

rule.statement: Type the public boundary first: services/*.py and route handlers, then internal helpers. Use `X | None` instead of `Optional[X]`. Run `mypy --strict` before commit.
rule.rationale: Public boundary is the highest-leverage place for types; PEP 604 `|` syntax is the canonical form in 3.10+.

rule.statement: Use ruff for linting and formatting (`ruff check --fix && ruff format`). Do NOT use black/isort/flake8 — they conflict with ruff.
rule.rationale: Tool consolidation eliminates rule-conflict and saves CI time.

rule.statement: Require Python 3.11+ in `pyproject.toml` (`python = "^3.11"`). Drop 3.10 — TaskGroup needs 3.11.
rule.rationale: 3.11 brings TaskGroup, ExceptionGroup, faster startup; below it concurrency patterns are awkward.

rule.statement: Use `X | None` over `Optional[X]`, and `list[int]` over `List[int]`. Avoid `from typing import List, Dict, Optional` in 3.11+.
rule.rationale: PEP 604 + PEP 585 made these forms native; the typing-module aliases are legacy.

rule.statement: services/ layer present, asyncio.gather for concurrent I/O, mypy --strict on public boundary, ruff replacing black/isort/flake8, Python 3.11+.
Python < 3.11 or single-file scripts where overhead exceeds value.
